name: django-annoying

metadata.name: django-annoying
metadata.summary: This is a django application that tries to eliminate annoying things in the Django framework.

documentation:
**Features:**

            - render_to decorator - Reduce typing in django views.
            - signals decorator - Allow using signals as decorators.
            - ajax_request decorator - Returns JsonResponse with dict as content.
            - autostrip decorator - Strip form text fields before validation.
            - get_object_or_None function - Similar to get_object_or_404, but returns None if the object is not found.
            - get_config function - Get settings from django.conf if exists, return a default value otherwise.
            - AutoOneToOne field - Creates related object on first call if it doesn't exist yet.
            - HttpResponseReload - Reload and stay on same page from where request was made.
            - StaticServer middleware - Instead of configuring urls.py, just add this middleware and it will serve you static files.
            - JSONField - A field that stores a Python object as JSON and retrieves it as a Python object.


        **Installation instructions:**

             - Copy the "annoying" directory to your Django project or put it in your PYTHONPATH.
             - You can also run "python setup.py install", "easy_install django-annoying", or "pip install django-annoying".


        **Download:**

            - git clone git://github.com/skorokithakis/django-annoying.git
            - hg clone http://bitbucket.org/Stavros/django-annoying/

methods.name: tests.test_fields.FieldsTestCase.test_auto_one_to_one_race_condition
documentation:
Test for case:

process #1 checks if related object exists
process #2 checks if related object exists
process #1 creates related object
process #2 returns object created by process #1

classes.name: annoying.middlewares.StaticServe
documentation: Django middleware for serving static files instead of using urls.py

classes.name: annoying.middlewares.RedirectMiddleware
documentation:
You must add this middleware to MIDDLEWARE_CLASSES list,
to make work Redirect exception. All arguments passed to
Redirect will be passed to django built in redirect function.

functions.name: annoying.functions.get_object_or_None
documentation:
Uses get() to return an object or None if the object does not exist.

klass may be a Model, Manager, or QuerySet object. All other passed
arguments and keyword arguments are used in the get() query.

Note: Like with get(), a MultipleObjectsReturned will be raised if more than one
object is found.

functions.name: annoying.functions.get_config
documentation:
Get settings from django.conf if exists,
return default value otherwise

example:

ADMIN_EMAIL = get_config('ADMIN_EMAIL', 'default@email.com')

functions.name: annoying.functions.get_object_or_this
documentation:
Uses get() to return an object or the value of <this> argument
if object does not exist.

If the <this> argument if not provided None would be returned.
<model> can be either a QuerySet instance or a class.

classes.name: annoying.fields.AutoSingleRelatedObjectDescriptor
documentation: The descriptor that handles the object creation for an AutoOneToOneField.

classes.name: annoying.fields.AutoOneToOneField
documentation:
OneToOneField creates related object on first call if it doesnt exist yet.
Use it instead of original OneToOne field.

example:

    class MyProfile(models.Model):
        user = AutoOneToOneField(User, primary_key=True)
        home_page = models.URLField(max_length=255, blank=True)
        icq = models.IntegerField(max_length=255, null=True)

classes.name: annoying.fields.JSONField

methods.name: annoying.fields.JSONField.to_python
documentation: Convert a string from the database to a Python value.

methods.name: annoying.fields.JSONField.get_prep_value
documentation: Convert the value to a string so it can be stored in the database.

JSONField is a generic textfield that neatly serializes/unserializes
JSON objects seamlessly.
Django snippet #1478

Custom serializer/deserializer functions can be used to customize field's behavior.
Defaults:
 - serializer: json.dumps(value, cls=DjangoJSONEncoder, sort_keys=True, indent=2, separators=(',', ': '))
 - deserializer: json.loads(value)

example:
    class Page(models.Model):
        data = JSONField(blank=True, null=True)


    page = Page.objects.get(pk=5)
    page.data = {'title': 'test', 'type': 3}
    page.save()

annoying.decorators.autostrip
documentation:
strip text fields before validation

example:
@autostrip
class PersonForm(forms.Form):
    name = forms.CharField(min_length=2, max_length=10)
    email = forms.EmailField()

Author: nail.xx

classes.name: annoying.decorators.Signals
documentation:
Convenient wrapper for working with Django's signals (or any other
implementation using same API).

Example of usage:

```
# connect to registered signal
@signals.post_save(sender=YourModel)
def sighandler(instance, **kwargs):
    pass

# connect to any signal
signals.register_signal(siginstance, signame) # and then as in example above

or

@signals(siginstance, sender=YourModel)
def sighandler(instance, **kwargs):
    pass
```

In any case defined function will remain as is, without any changes.

3. 2008 Alexander Solovyov, new BSD License
